   127→# =============================================================================
   128→# AST Nodes
   129→# =============================================================================
   130→
   131→class ASTNode:
   132→    pass
   133→
   134→
   135→class SignalRef(ASTNode):
   136→    """Reference to a signal with optional slice/index.
   137→
   138→    name: signal name
   139→    msb: None for scalar reference, otherwise MSB of slice/index
   140→    lsb: None for scalar reference, LSB of slice (for single bit, msb=lsb)
   141→    """
   142→    def __init__(self, name: str, msb: Optional[int] = None, lsb: Optional[int] = None):
   143→        self.name = name
   144→        self.msb = msb
   145→        self.lsb = lsb
   146→
   147→    def __repr__(self):
   148→        if self.msb is None:
   149→            return f"SignalRef({self.name})"
   150→        elif self.msb == self.lsb:
   151→            return f"SignalRef({self.name}[{self.msb}])"
   152→        else:
   153→            return f"SignalRef({self.name}[{self.msb}:{self.lsb}])"
   154→
   155→
   156→class Literal(ASTNode):
   157→    """Literal value with width.
   158→
   159→    value: integer value
   160→    width: bit width
   161→    """
   162→    def __init__(self, value: int, width: int):
   163→        self.value = value
   164→        self.width = width
   165→
   166→    def __repr__(self):
   167→        return f"Literal(0b{self.value:0{self.width}b}, width={self.width})"
   168→
   169→
   170→class Concat(ASTNode):
   171→    """Concatenation: {e1, e2, ..., ek}"""
   172→    def __init__(self, parts: List[ASTNode]):
   173→        self.parts = parts
   174→
   175→    def __repr__(self):
   176→        return f"Concat([{', '.join(repr(p) for p in self.parts)}])"
   177→
   178→
   179→class OpCall(ASTNode):
   180→    """Operator call: OP(arg1, arg2, ...)"""
   181→    def __init__(self, op: str, args: List[ASTNode]):
   182→        self.op = op
   183→        self.args = args
   184→
   185→    def __repr__(self):
   186→        return f"OpCall({self.op}, [{', '.join(repr(a) for a in self.args)}])"
   187→
   188→
   189→# =============================================================================
   190→# Signal Declaration
   191→# =============================================================================
   192→
   193→class SignalDecl:
   194→    """Signal declaration with width information.
   195→
   196→    name: signal name
   197→    sig_type: 'input', 'output', or 'wire'
   198→    msb: MSB of the signal (None for scalar in old format)
   199→    lsb: LSB of the signal (None for scalar in old format)
   200→    """
   201→    def __init__(self, name: str, sig_type: str, msb: Optional[int] = None, lsb: Optional[int] = None):
   202→        self.name = name
   203→        self.sig_type = sig_type
   204→        self.msb = msb
   205→        self.lsb = lsb
   206→
   207→    @property
   208→    def width(self) -> int:
   209→        if self.msb is None:
   210→            return 1
   211→        return self.msb - self.lsb + 1
   212→
   213→    def __repr__(self):
   214→        if self.msb is None:
   215→            return f"SignalDecl({self.name}, {self.sig_type})"
   216→        return f"SignalDecl({self.name}[{self.msb}:{self.lsb}], {self.sig_type})"
   217→
   218→
   219→# =============================================================================
   220→# Token Types
   221→# =============================================================================
   222→
   223→TOKEN_ID = 'ID'
   224→TOKEN_KW = 'KW'
   225→TOKEN_OP = 'OP'
   226→TOKEN_LITERAL = 'LITERAL'
   227→TOKEN_NUMBER = 'NUMBER'
   228→TOKEN_LBRACE = '{'
   229→TOKEN_RBRACE = '}'
   230→TOKEN_LBRACK = '['
   231→TOKEN_RBRACK = ']'
   232→TOKEN_COLON = ':'
   233→TOKEN_EQ = '='
   234→TOKEN_COMMA = ','
   235→TOKEN_LPAREN = '('
   236→TOKEN_RPAREN = ')'
   237→TOKEN_EOF = 'EOF'
   238→
   239→
   240→class Token:
   241→    def __init__(self, type: str, value: str, line: int, col: int):
   242→        self.type = type
   243→        self.value = value
   244→        self.line = line
   245→        self.col = col
   246→
   247→    def __repr__(self):
   248→        return f"Token({self.type}, {self.value!r}, line={self.line}, col={self.col})"
   249→
   250→
   251→# =============================================================================
   252→# Lexer
   253→# =============================================================================
   254→
   255→def tokenize(line: str, line_num: int) -> List[Token]:
   256→    """Tokenize a single line.
   257→
   258→    Returns list of Token objects.
   259→    """
   260→    tokens = []
   261→    pos = 0
   262→    length = len(line)
   263→
   264→    # Operator patterns (case-insensitive)
   265→    operators = {'AND', 'OR', 'XOR', 'NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR', 'NOT', 'BUF',
   266→                 'MUX', 'ITE', 'REDUCE_AND', 'REDUCE_OR', 'REDUCE_XOR', 'EQ'}
   267→
   268→    while pos < length:
   269→        col_pos = pos + 1  # 1-based column
   270→
   271→        # Skip whitespace
   272→        if line[pos] in ' \t':
   273→            pos += 1
   274→            continue
   275→
   276→        # Comment
   277→        if line[pos] == '#':
   278→            break  # Rest of line is comment, ignore
   279→
   280→        # Identifiers (letters, underscores, digits after first char)
   281→        if re.match(r'[a-zA-Z_]', line[pos:]):
   282→            match = re.match(r'[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*', line[pos:])
   283→            if match:
   284→                token = match.group()
   285→                upper_token = token.upper()
   286→                if upper_token in operators:
   287→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_OP, upper_token, line_num, col_pos))
   288→                elif upper_token in {'INPUT', 'OUTPUT', 'WIRE'}:
   289→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_KW, upper_token, line_num, col_pos))
   290→                else:
   291→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_ID, token, line_num, col_pos))
   292→                pos += len(token)
   293→                continue
   294→
   295→        # Numbers (decimal integers for msb:lsb notation)
   296→        if re.match(r'[0-9]', line[pos:]):
   297→            match = re.match(r'[0-9]+', line[pos:])
   298→            if match:
   299→                tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_NUMBER, match.group(), line_num, col_pos))
   300→                pos += len(match.group())
   301→                continue
   302→
   303→        # Literals: 0, 1 (scalar), sized/unsized vectors
   304→        if line[pos] in '01':
   305→            # Check if this is a sized literal like 8'b1010
   306→            rest = line[pos:]
   307→            sized_match = re.match(r'(\d+)\'[bBhHdD]([\d01a-fA-F_]+)', rest)
   308→            if sized_match:
   309→                # This is a sized literal
   310→                width = int(sized_match.group(1))
   311→                radix_str = sized_match.group(2).lower()
   312→                value_str = sized_match.group(3).replace('_', '')
   313→
   314→                # Parse based on radix
   315→                if radix_str in ('b', 'h', 'd'):
   316→                    try:
   317→                        radix_map = {'b': 2, 'h': 16, 'd': 10}
   318→                        value = int(value_str, radix_map[radix_str])
   319→                    except ValueError:
   320→                        raise CircParseError(
   321→                            f"Invalid literal: {sized_match.group(0)}",
   322→                            file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   323→                        )
   324→
   325→                    # Check value fits in width
   326→                    if value >= (1 << width):
   327→                        raise CircParseError(
   328→                            f"Value {value} too large for {width}-bit literal",
   329→                            file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   330→                        )
   331→
   332→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_LITERAL, f"{width}'{radix_str}{value_str}", line_num, col_pos))
   333→                    pos += len(sized_match.group(0))
   334→                    continue
   335→            else:
   336→                # Scalar literal
   337→                tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_LITERAL, line[pos], line_num, col_pos))
   338→                pos += 1
   339→                continue
   340→
   341→        # Check for 0b or 0x binary/hex literals
   342→        if line[pos:pos+2] in ('0b', '0x'):
   343→            prefix = line[pos:pos+2]
   344→            rest = line[pos+2:]
   345→            # Must have at least one digit after prefix
   346→            match = re.match(r'[0-9a-fA-F]+', rest)
   347→            if match:
   348→                digits = match.group()
   349→                tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_LITERAL, f"{prefix}{digits}", line_num, col_pos))
   350→                pos += 2 + len(digits)
   351→                continue
   352→            else:
   353→                raise CircParseError(
   354→                    f"Binary/hex literal must have at least one digit",
   355→                    file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   356→                )
   357→
   358→        # Braces and brackets
   359→        if line[pos] in '{}[]':
   360→            tokens.append(Token(line[pos], line[pos], line_num, col_pos))
   361→            pos += 1
   362→            continue
   363→
   364→        # Colon
   365→        if line[pos] == ':':
   366→            tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_COLON, ':', line_num, col_pos))
   367→            pos += 1
   368→            continue
   369→
   370→        # Comma
   371→        if line[pos] == ',':
   372→            tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_COMMA, ',', line_num, col_pos))
   373→            pos += 1
   374→            continue
   375→
   376→        # Equals sign
   377→        if line[pos] == '=':
   378→            tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_EQ, '=', line_num, col_pos))
   379→            pos += 1
   380→            continue
   381→
   382→        # Parentheses
   383→        if line[pos] in '()':
   384→            type_map = {'(': TOKEN_LPAREN, ')': TOKEN_RPAREN}
   385→            tokens.append(Token(type_map[line[pos]], line[pos], line_num, col_pos))
   386→            pos += 1
   387→            continue
   388→
   389→        # Unknown character
   390→        raise CircParseError(
   391→            f"Unexpected character: {line[pos]}",
   392→            file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   393→        )
   394→
   395→    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_EOF, '', line_num, len(line) + 1))
   396→    return tokens
   397→
   398→
   399→# =============================================================================
   400→# Parser
   401→# =============================================================================
   402→
   403→class Parser:
   404→    """Parser for circuit expressions."""
   405→
   406→    def __init__(self, tokens: List[Token], filepath: str):
   407→        self.tokens = tokens
   408→        self.filepath = filepath
   409→        self.pos = 0
   410→
   411→    def peek(self) -> Token:
   412→        return self.tokens[self.pos]
   413→
   414→    def consume(self, expected_type: Optional[str] = None,
   415→                expected_value: Optional[str] = None) -> Token:
   416→        tok = self.peek()
   417→        if expected_type is not None and tok.type != expected_type:
   418→            raise self.error(f"Expected token type {expected_type}, got {tok.type}")
   419→        if expected_value is not None and tok.value != expected_value:
   420→            raise self.error(f"Expected {expected_value}, got {tok.value}")
   421→        self.pos += 1
   422→        return tok
   423→
   424→    def error(self, message: str) -> CircParseError:
   425→        tok = self.peek()
   426→        return CircParseError(
   427→            message,
   428→            file=self.filepath,
   429→            line=tok.line,
   430→            col=tok.col
   431→        )
   432→
   433→    def parse_expression(self) -> ASTNode:
   434→        """Parse an expression: concatenation, signal ref, literal, or op call."""
   435→        tok = self.peek()
   436→
   437→        # If it's an identifier followed by [, it's a slice/index
   438→        if tok.type == TOKEN_ID:
   439→            name = tok.value
   440→            self.consume(TOKEN_ID)
   441→
   442→            # Check for slice or index
   443→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_LBRACK:
   444→                return self.parse_signal_ref_with_select(name)
   445→            else:
   446→                return SignalRef(name)
   447→
   448→        elif tok.type == TOKEN_LITERAL:
   449→            return self.parse_literal()
   450→
   451→        elif tok.type == TOKEN_LBRACE:
   452→            # Concatenation: {e1, e2, ...}
   453→            return self.parse_concat()
   454→
   455→        elif tok.type == TOKEN_OP:
   456→            # Operator call: OP(...)
   457→            return self.parse_op_call()
   458→
   459→        else:
   460→            raise self.error(f"Unexpected token in expression: {tok.value}")
   461→
   462→    def parse_signal_ref_with_select(self, name: str) -> SignalRef:
   463→        """Parse a signal reference with optional slice/index: v[i] or v[hi:lo]"""
   464→        self.consume(TOKEN_LBRACK)
   465→
   466→        # Parse first number (msb or index)
   467→        if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   468→            raise self.error("Expected number in slice/index")
   469→        msb_val = int(self.consume().value)
   470→
   471→        # Check if it's a slice (with colon) or single bit index
   472→        if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COLON:
   473→            self.consume(TOKEN_COLON)  # consume ':'
   474→
   475→            # Parse lsb
   476→            if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   477→                raise self.error("Expected number after colon in slice")
   478→            lsb_val = int(self.consume().value)
   479→
   480→            self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACK)
   481→
   482→            # Validate: msb >= lsb >= 0
   483→            if msb_val < lsb_val:
   484→                raise self.error(f"Invalid slice: msb ({msb_val}) < lsb ({lsb_val})")
   485→            if lsb_val < 0:
   486→                raise self.error(f"Invalid slice: lsb ({lsb_val}) < 0")
   487→
   488→            return SignalRef(name, msb_val, lsb_val)
   489→        else:
   490→            # Single bit index
   491→            self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACK)
   492→            return SignalRef(name, msb_val, msb_val)  # msb=lsb for single bit
   493→
   494→    def parse_literal(self) -> Literal:
   495→        """Parse a literal value."""
   496→        tok = self.consume(TOKEN_LITERAL)
   497→        val = tok.value
   498→
   499→        if val in ('0', '1'):
   500→            return Literal(int(val), 1)
   501→        elif val.startswith('0b'):
   502→            # Binary literal: 0b1101
   503→            bits = val[2:]
   504→            if not bits:
   505→                raise self.error("Binary literal must have at least one digit")
   506→            value = int(bits, 2)
   507→            return Literal(value, len(bits))
   508→        elif val.startswith('0x'):
   509→            # Hex literal: 0x0f
   510→            hex_digits = val[2:]
   511→            if not hex_digits:
   512→                raise self.error("Hex literal must have at least one digit")
   513→            value = int(hex_digits, 16)
   514→            return Literal(value, len(hex_digits) * 4)
   515→        else:
   516→            # Sized literal: 8'b1010, 8'hff, 8'd200
   517→            match = re.match(r'(\d+)\'[bBhHdD]([\d01a-fA-F_]+)', val)
   518→            if match:
   519→                width = int(match.group(1))
   520→                radix_str = match.group(2).lower()
   521→                value_str = match.group(3).replace('_', '')
   522→
   523→                try:
   524→                    radix_map = {'b': 2, 'h': 16, 'd': 10}
   525→                    value = int(value_str, radix_map[radix_str])
   526→                except ValueError:
   527→                    raise self.error(f"Invalid literal: {val}")
   528→
   529→                if value >= (1 << width):
   530→                    raise self.error(f"Value {value} too large for {width}-bit literal")
   531→
   532→                return Literal(value, width)
   533→            else:
   534→                raise self.error(f"Invalid literal format: {val}")
   535→
   536→    def parse_concat(self) -> Concat:
   537→        """Parse concatenation: {e1, e2, ...}"""
   538→        self.consume(TOKEN_LBRACE)
   539→
   540→        parts = []
   541→        while True:
   542→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_RBRACE:
   543→                break
   544→
   545→            parts.append(self.parse_expression())
   546→
   547→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COMMA:
   548→                self.consume(TOKEN_COMMA)
   549→                continue
   550→            elif self.peek().type == TOKEN_RBRACE:
   551→                break
   552→
   553→        self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACE)
   554→        return Concat(parts)
   555→
   556→    def parse_op_call(self) -> OpCall:
   557→        """Parse operator call: OP(arg1, arg2, ...)"""
   558→        op_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_OP)
   559→        op = op_tok.value
   560→
   561→        self.consume(TOKEN_LPAREN)
   562→
   563→        args = []
   564→        while True:
   565→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_RPAREN:
   566→                break
   567→
   568→            args.append(self.parse_expression())
   569→
   570→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COMMA:
   571→                self.consume(TOKEN_COMMA)
   572→                continue
   573→            elif self.peek().type == TOKEN_RPAREN:
   574→                break
   575→
   576→        self.consume(TOKEN_RPAREN)
   577→        return OpCall(op, args)
   578→
   579→    def parse_assignment_line(self) -> Tuple[str, ASTNode]:
   580→        """Parse an assignment line: lhs = expression"""
   581→        # Parse LHS (identifier)
   582→        lhs_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_ID)
   583→        lhs = lhs_tok.value
   584→
   585→        self.consume(TOKEN_EQ)
   586→
   587→        # Parse RHS expression
   588→        expr = self.parse_expression()
   589→
   590→        # Make sure we're at EOF
   591→        if self.peek().type != TOKEN_EOF:
   592→            raise self.error(f"Unexpected token after expression: {self.peek().value}")
   593→
   594→        return lhs, expr
   595→
   596→    def parse_declaration(self) -> List[SignalDecl]:
   597→        """Parse a declaration line: INPUT/OUTPUT/WIRE name[msb:lsb]"""
   598→        kw_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_KW)
   599→        decl_type = kw_tok.value.lower()
   600→
   601→        decls = []
   602→        while True:
   603→            # Parse identifier
   604→            id_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_ID)
   605→            name = id_tok.value
   606→
   607→            # Check for width specifier
   608→            msb = None
   609→            lsb = None
   610→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_LBRACK:
   611→                self.consume(TOKEN_LBRACK)
   612→
   613→                # Parse msb
   614→                if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   615→                    raise self.error(f"Expected number after [ for {name}")
   616→                msb = int(self.consume().value)
   617→
   618→                # Check for slice or single bit
   619→                if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COLON:
   620→                    self.consume(TOKEN_COLON)
   621→
   622→                    if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   623→                        raise self.error(f"Expected number after : for {name}")
   624→                    lsb = int(self.consume().value)
   625→
   626→                    if msb < lsb:
   627→                        raise self.error(f"Invalid range for {name}: msb ({msb}) < lsb ({lsb})")
   628→                    if lsb < 0:
   629→                        raise self.error(f"Invalid range for {name}: lsb ({lsb}) < 0")
   630→                else:
   631→                    # Single bit - msb=lsb
   632→                    lsb = msb
   633→
   634→                self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACK)
   635→
   636→            decls.append(SignalDecl(name, decl_type, msb, lsb))
   637→
   638→            # Check for more names or end of line
   639→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_EOF:
   640→                break
   641→            elif self.peek().type == TOKEN_COMMA:
   642→                self.consume(TOKEN_COMMA)
   643→                continue
   644→            else:
   645→                raise self.error(f"Unexpected token: {self.peek().value}")
   646→
   647→        return decls
   648→
   649→
   650→def parse_circ_file(filepath: str) -> Tuple[List[SignalDecl], List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]]:
   651→    """Parse a .circ file and return (declarations, assignments).
   652→
   653→    Returns:
   654→        declarations: List of SignalDecl objects
   655→        assignments: List of (lhs, expression) tuples
   656→    """
   657→    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
   658→        lines = f.readlines()
   659→
   660→    declarations = []
   661→    assignments = []
   662→    seen_assignment = False
   663→
   664→    for line_num, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
   665→        line = line.rstrip('\n\r')
   666→
   667→        # Skip empty lines and comments
   668→        stripped = line.lstrip()
   669→        if not stripped or stripped.startswith('#'):
   670→            continue
   671→
   672→        try:
   673→            tokens = tokenize(line, line_num)
   674→        except CircParseError as e:
   675→            if e.file is None:
   676→                e.file = filepath
   677→            raise
   678→
   679→        if not tokens:
   680→            continue
   681→
   682→        parser = Parser(tokens, filepath)
   683→
   684→        # Check if it's a declaration
   685→        if tokens[0].type == TOKEN_KW:
   686→            # Declaration line
   687→            if seen_assignment:
   688→                raise DeclarationAfterAssignmentError(
   689→                    f"Declaration found after assignment",
   690→                    file=filepath,
   691→                    line=line_num,
   692→                    col=1
   693→                )
   694→
   695→            try:
   696→                decls = parser.parse_declaration()
   697→                declarations.extend(decls)
   698→            except CircParseError as e:
   699→                if e.file is None:
   700→                    e.file = filepath
   701→                raise
   702→
   703→        elif tokens[0].type == TOKEN_ID:
   704→            # Assignment line
   705→            seen_assignment = True
   706→
   707→            try:
   708��                lhs, expr = parser.parse_assignment_line()
   709→                assignments.append((lhs, expr))
   710→            except CircParseError as e:
   711→                if e.file is None:
   712→                    e.file = filepath
   713→                raise
   714→        else:
   715→            raise CircParseError(
   716→                f"Expected keyword (input/output/wire) or identifier",
   717→                file=filepath,
   718→                line=line_num,
   719→                col=1
   720→            )
   721→
   722→    return declarations, assignments
   723→

   725→# =============================================================================
   726→# JSON Circuit Format Parser
   727→# =============================================================================
   728→
   729→def parse_json_file(filepath: str) -> Tuple[List[SignalDecl], List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]]:
   730→    """Parse a JSON circuit file and return (declarations, assignments).
   731→
   732→    Returns:
   733→        declarations: List of SignalDecl objects
   734→        assignments: List of (lhs, expression) tuples
   735→
   736→    Raises:
   737→        JsonParseError: Invalid JSON syntax
   738→        JsonSchemaError: JSON doesn't match expected schema
   739→        CircParseError: Expression parsing error
   740→    """
   741→    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
   742→        try:
   743→            data = json.load(f)
   744→        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
   745→            raise JsonParseError(
   746→                f"Invalid JSON syntax: {e}",
   747→                file=filepath,
   748→                line=e.lineno,
   749→                col=e.colno
   750→            )
   751→
   752→    # Validate schema
   753→    if not isinstance(data, dict):
   754→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   755→            "JSON root must be an object",
   756→            file=filepath
   757→        )
   758→
   759→    required_keys = {'format_version', 'inputs', 'outputs', 'wires', 'assignments'}
   760→    missing = required_keys - set(data.keys())
   761→    if missing:
   762→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   763→            f"Missing required keys: {', '.join(sorted(missing))}",
   764→            file=filepath
   765→        )
   766→
   767→    # Check format_version
   768→    if data['format_version'] != 1:
   769→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   770→            f"Unsupported format_version: {data['format_version']}. Expected 1.",
   771→            file=filepath
   772→        )
   773→
   774→    # Helper to validate port object
   775→    def validate_port(port: dict, key_name: str) -> dict:
   776→        if not isinstance(port, dict):
   777→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   778→                f"{key_name} must be an array of objects",
   779→                file=filepath
   780→            )
   781→        if 'name' not in port:
   782→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   783→                f"Port object missing 'name' field",
   784→                file=filepath
   785→            )
   786→        msb = port.get('msb')
   787→        lsb = port.get('lsb')
   788→
   789→        if msb is None and lsb is None:
   790→            # Scalar port - valid
   791→            return port
   792→
   793→        if not isinstance(msb, int) or not isinstance(lsb, int):
   794→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   795→                f"Port '{port.get('name', '?')}': msb and lsb must be integers",
   796→                file=filepath
   797→            )
   798→
   799→        if msb < lsb:
   800→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   801→                f"Port '{port['name']}': msb ({msb}) < lsb ({lsb})",
   802→                file=filepath
   803→            )
   804→
   805→        if lsb < 0:
   806→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   807→                f"Port '{port['name']}': lsb ({lsb}) < 0",
   808→                file=filepath
   809→            )
   810→
   811→        return port
   812→
   813→    # Parse inputs
   814→    inputs_list = data['inputs']
   815→    if not isinstance(inputs_list, list):
   816→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   817→            "'inputs' must be an array",
   818→            file=filepath
   819→        )
   820→
   821→    declarations: List[SignalDecl] = []
   822→    seen_names: Set[str] = set()
   823→
   824→    for i, port in enumerate(inputs_list):
   825→        validate_port(port, f"inputs[{i}]")
   826→        name = port['name']
   827→        if name in seen_names:
   828→            raise DuplicateNameError(
   829→                f"Duplicate input name: {name}",
   830→                file=filepath
   831→            )
   832→        seen_names.add(name)
   833→
   834→        msb = port.get('msb')
   835→        lsb = port.get('lsb')
   836→        # Convert None/None to None, None for scalar; otherwise keep values
   837→        msb_val = msb if msb is not None else None
   838→        lsb_val = lsb if lsb is not None else None
   839→        declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'input', msb_val, lsb_val))
   840→
   841→    # Parse outputs
   842→    outputs_list = data['outputs']
   843→    if not isinstance(outputs_list, list):
   844→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   845→            "'outputs' must be an array",
   846→            file=filepath
   847→        )
   848→
   849→    for i, port in enumerate(outputs_list):
   850→        validate_port(port, f"outputs[{i}]")
   851→        name = port['name']
   852→        if name in seen_names:
   853→            raise DuplicateNameError(
   854→                f"Duplicate output name: {name}",
   855→                file=filepath
   856→            )
   857→        seen_names.add(name)
   858→
   859→        msb = port.get('msb')
   860→        lsb = port.get('lsb')
   861→        msb_val = msb if msb is not None else None
   862→        lsb_val = lsb if lsb is not None else None
   863→        declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'output', msb_val, lsb_val))
   864→
   865→    # Parse wires
   866→    wires_list = data['wires']
   867→    if not isinstance(wires_list, list):
   868→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   869→            "'wires' must be an array",
   870→            file=filepath
   871→        )
   872→
   873→    for i, port in enumerate(wires_list):
   874→        validate_port(port, f"wires[{i}]")
   875→        name = port['name']
   876→        if name in seen_names:
   877→            raise DuplicateNameError(
   878→                f"Duplicate wire name: {name}",
   879→                file=filepath
   880→            )
   881→        seen_names.add(name)
   882→
   883→        msb = port.get('msb')
   884→        lsb = port.get('lsb')
   885→        msb_val = msb if msb is not None else None
   886→        lsb_val = lsb if lsb is not None else None
   887→        declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'wire', msb_val, lsb_val))
   888→
   889→    # Parse assignments
   890→    assignments_list = data['assignments']
   891→    if not isinstance(assignments_list, list):
   892→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   893→            "'assignments' must be an array",
   894→            file=filepath
   895→        )
   896→
   897→    assignments: List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]] = []
   898→    lhs_seen: Set[str] = set()
   899→
   900→    for i, assn in enumerate(assignments_list):
   901→        if not isinstance(assn, dict):
   902→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   903→                f"assignments[{i}] must be an object",
   904→                file=filepath
   905→            )
   906→        if 'lhs' not in assn:
   907→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   908→                f"assignments[{i}] missing 'lhs' field",
   909→                file=filepath
   910→            )
   911→        if 'rhs' not in assn:
   912→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   913→                f"assignments[{i}] missing 'rhs' field",
   914→                file=filepath
   915→            )
   916→
   917→        lhs = assn['lhs']
   918→        rhs_str = assn['rhs']
   919→
   920→        if not isinstance(lhs, str):
   921→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   922→                f"assignments[{i}].lhs must be a string",
   923→                file=filepath
   924→            )
   925→        if not isinstance(rhs_str, str):
   926→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   927→                f"assignments[{i}].rhs must be a string",
   928→                file=filepath
   929→            )
   930→
   931→        if lhs in lhs_seen:
   932→            raise RedefinitionError(
   933→                f"Signal '{lhs}' defined multiple times",
   934→                file=filepath
   935→            )
   936→        lhs_seen.add(lhs)
   937→
   938→        # Parse RHS expression using existing expression parser
   939→        # Create a temporary tokens list by tokenizing the expression string
   940→        # We'll reuse the existing tokenize function by creating a mock line
   941→        try:
   942→            tokens = tokenize(rhs_str, 1)  # line 1 for expression error context
   943→        except CircParseError as e:
   944→            # Adjust line/col to point to the assignment
   945→            raise CircParseError(
   946→                f"Error in assignment {i}: {e.message}",
   947→                file=filepath,
   948→                line=None,
   949→                col=None
   950→            )
   951→
   952→        parser = Parser(tokens, filepath)
   953→        try:
   954→            expr = parser.parse_expression()
   955→        except CircParseError as e:
   956→            raise CircParseError(
   957→                f"Error in assignment {i}: {e.message}",
   958→                file=filepath
   959→            )
   960→
   961→        # Ensure we consumed all tokens (no trailing junk)
   962→        if parser.peek().type != TOKEN_EOF:
   963→            raise CircParseError(
   964→                f"Unexpected token after expression in assignment {i}",
   965→                file=filepath
   966→            )
   967→
   968→        assignments.append((lhs, expr))
   969→
   970→    # Validate that every wire and output appears exactly once as assignment LHS
   971→    wire_names = {d.name for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'wire'}
   972→    output_names = {d.name for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'output'}
   973→    assigned_names = set(lhs_seen)
   974→
   975→    unassigned = (wire_names | output_names) - assigned_names
   976→    if unassigned:
   977→        raise UnassignedSignalError(
   978→            f"Unassigned signals: {', '.join(sorted(unassigned))}",
   979→            file=filepath
   980→        )
   981→
   982→    # Check for extra assignments (LHS not declared)
   983→    declared_names = {d.name for d in declarations}
   984→    extra_assigned = assigned_names - declared_names
   985→    if extra_assigned:
   986→        raise UndefinedNameError(
   987→            f"Assignment to undeclared signal(s): {', '.join(sorted(extra_assigned))}",
   988→            file=filepath
   989→        )
   990→
   991→    return declarations, assignments
   992→

   997→
   998→def parse_bench_file(filepath: str) -> Tuple[List[SignalDecl], List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]]:
   999→    """Parse a BENCH format circuit file and return (declarations, assignments).
  1000→
  1001→    BENCH is a scalar-only format commonly used in circuit benchmarks.
  1002→
  1003→    Returns:
  1004→        declarations: List of SignalDecl objects (all scalar)
  1005→        assignments: List of (lhs, expression) tuples
  1006→
  1007→    Raises:
  1008→        BenchParseError: Syntax error in .bench file
  1009→        RedefinitionError: Signal defined multiple times
  1010→        UndefinedNameError: Undefined name referenced
  1011→    """
  1012→    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
  1013→        lines = f.readlines()
  1014→
  1015→    declarations: List[SignalDecl] = []
  1016→    assignments: List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]] = []
  1017→
  1018→    # Track all signals (inputs, wires, outputs)
  1019→    signal_names: Set[str] = set()
  1020→    input_names: Set[str] = set()
  1021→    output_names: Set[str] = set()
  1022→    wire_names: Set[str] = set()
  1023→
  1024→    # Track assigned signals (for RedefinitionError)
  1025→    assigned_lhs: Set[str] = set()
  1026→
  1027→    # For expression parsing
  1028→    op_arity = {
  1029→        'NOT': 1,
  1030→        'BUF': 1,
  1031→        'AND': 2,
  1032→        'OR': 2,
  1033→        'XOR': 2,
  1034→        'NAND': 2,
  1035→        'NOR': 2,
  1036→        'XNOR': 2,
  1037→    }
  1038→
  1039→    for line_num, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
  1040→        line = line.rstrip('\n\r')
  1041→
  1042→        # Strip comment
  1043→        if '#' in line:
  1044→            line = line[:line.index('#')]
  1045→
  1046→        # Skip empty lines
  1047→        stripped = line.strip()
  1048→        if not stripped:
  1049→            continue
  1050→
  1051→        # Remove all whitespace for easier parsing
  1052→        no_whitespace = ''.join(stripped.split())
  1053→
  1054→        # Check for INPUT(name)
  1055→        if no_whitespace.startswith('INPUT(') and no_whitespace.endswith(')'):
  1056→            name = no_whitespace[6:-1]  # Remove 'INPUT(' and ')'
  1057→            if not name:
  1058→                raise BenchParseError(
  1059→                    f"Empty input name at line {line_num}",
  1060→                    file=filepath,
  1061→                    line=line_num,
  1062→                    col=1
  1063→                )
  1064→
  1065→            # Check for brackets (vector support not allowed in BENCH)
  1066→            if '[' in name or ']' in name:
  1067→                raise BenchParseError(
  1068→                    f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {name}",
  1069→                    file=filepath,
  1070→                    line=line_num,
  1071→                    col=1
  1072→                )
  1073→
  1074→            if name in signal_names:
  1075→                raise RedefinitionError(
  1076→                    f"Duplicate signal definition: {name}",
  1077→                    file=filepath,
  1078→                    line=line_num,
  1079→                    col=1
  1080→                )
  1081→
  1082→            signal_names.add(name)
  1083→            input_names.add(name)
  1084→            declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'input', None, None))
  1085→            continue
  1086→
  1087→        # Check for OUTPUT(name)
  1088→        if no_whitespace.startswith('OUTPUT(') and no_whitespace.endswith(')'):
  1089→            name = no_whitespace[7:-1]  # Remove 'OUTPUT(' and ')'
  1090→            if not name:
  1091→                raise BenchParseError(
  1092→                    f"Empty output name at line {line_num}",
  1093→                    file=filepath,
  1094→                    line=line_num,
  1095→                    col=1
  1096→                )
  1097→
  1098→            if '[' in name or ']' in name:
  1099→                raise BenchParseError(
  1100→                    f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {name}",
  1101→                    file=filepath,
  1102→                    line=line_num,
  1103→                    col=1
  1104→                )
  1105→
  1106→            if name in signal_names:
  1107→                raise RedefinitionError(
  1108→                    f"Duplicate signal definition: {name}",
  1109→                    file=filepath,
  1110→                    line=line_num,
  1111→                    col=1
  1112→                )
  1113→
  1114→            signal_names.add(name)
  1115→            output_names.add(name)
  1116→            # Add as output declaration
  1117→            declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'output', None, None))
  1118→            continue
  1119→
  1120→        # Check for assignment: lhs = OP(arg1, arg2, ...)
  1121→        if '=' in no_whitespace:
  1122→            parts = no_whitespace.split('=', 1)
  1123→            if len(parts) != 2:
  1124→                raise BenchParseError(
  1125→                    f"Invalid assignment at line {line_num}",
  1126→                    file=filepath,
  1127→                    line=line_num,
  1128→                    col=1
  1129→                )
  1130→
  1131→            lhs = parts[0]
  1132→            rhs = parts[1]
  1133→
  1134→            # Validate LHS
  1135→            if not lhs:
  1136→                raise BenchParseError(
  1137→                    f"Empty LHS in assignment at line {line_num}",
  1138→                    file=filepath,
  1139→                    line=line_num,
  1140→                    col=1
  1141→                )
  1142→
  1143→            if '[' in lhs or ']' in lhs:
  1144→                raise BenchParseError(
  1145→                    f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {lhs}",
  1146→                    file=filepath,
  1147→                    line=line_num,
  1148→                    col=1
  1149→                )
  1150→
  1151→            # Check for redefinition
  1152→            if lhs in assigned_lhs:
  1153→                raise RedefinitionError(
  1154→                    f"Redefinition of signal: {lhs}",
  1155→                    file=filepath,
  1156→                    line=line_num,
  1157→                    col=1
  1158→                )
  1159→
  1160→            assigned_lhs.add(lhs)
  1161→
  1162→            # Add as wire if not already declared as input/output
  1163→            if lhs not in signal_names:
  1164→                signal_names.add(lhs)
  1165→                wire_names.add(lhs)
  1166→                declarations.append(SignalDecl(lhs, 'wire', None, None))
  1167→
  1168→            # Parse RHS: OP(arg1, arg2, ...)
  1169→            # Find operator name
  1170→            op_match = None
  1171→            for op in op_arity:
  1172→                if rhs.startswith(op + '(') and rhs.endswith(')'):
  1173→                    op_match = op
  1174→                    break
  1175→
  1176→            if op_match is None:
  1177→                raise BenchParseError(
  1178→                    f"Unknown or malformed operator call: {rhs}",
  1179→                    file=filepath,
  1180→                    line=line_num,
  1181→                    col=1
  1182→                )
  1183→
  1184→            args_str = rhs[len(op_match)+1:-1]  # Remove OP( and )
  1185→            if args_str:
  1186→                arg_names = [arg.strip() for arg in args_str.split(',')]
  1187→            else:
  1188→                arg_names = []
  1189→
  1190→            # Validate argument count
  1191→            min_arity = op_arity[op_match]
  1192→            if len(arg_names) < min_arity:
  1193→                raise BenchParseError(
  1194→                    f"Operator {op_match} requires at least {min_arity} operands, got {len(arg_names)}",
  1195→                    file=filepath,
  1196→                    line=line_num,
  1197→                    col=1
  1198→                )
  1199→
  1200→            # Check for literals (BENCH doesn't allow literals in RHS)
  1201→            for arg in arg_names:
  1202→                # Literals: 0, 1, 0b..., 0x..., sized literals
  1203→                if arg in ('0', '1'):
  1204→                    raise BenchParseError(
  1205→                        f"Literals not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
  1206→                        file=filepath,
  1207→                        line=line_num,
  1208→                        col=1
  1209→                    )
  1210→                if arg.startswith('0b') or arg.startswith('0x'):
  1211→                    raise BenchParseError(
  1212→                        f"Literals not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
  1213→                        file=filepath,
  1214→                        line=line_num,
  1215→                        col=1
  1216→                    )
  1217→                if re.match(r'^\d+[\'[bBhHdD]', arg):
  1218→                    raise BenchParseError(
  1219→                        f"Literals not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
  1220→                        file=filepath,
  1221→                        line=line_num,
  1222→                        col=1
  1223→                    )
  1224→
  1225→                # Check for brackets (vector not allowed)
  1226→                if '[' in arg or ']' in arg:
  1227→                    raise BenchParseError(
  1228→                        f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
  1229→                        file=filepath,
  1230→                        line=line_num,
  1231→                        col=1
  1232→                    )
  1233→
  1234→            # Build AST: OpCall with SignalRef arguments
  1235→            args_ast = []
  1236→            for arg in arg_names:
  1237→                # BENCH is scalar-only, no indices/slices
  1238→                args_ast.append(SignalRef(arg))
  1239→
  1240→            expr = OpCall(op_match, args_ast)
  1241→            assignments.append((lhs, expr))
  1242→            continue
  1243→
  1244→        # If we get here, the line is invalid
  1245→        raise BenchParseError(
  1246→            f"Invalid statement: {stripped}",
  1247→            file=filepath,
  1248→            line=line_num,
  1249→            col=1
  1250→        )
  1251→
  1252→    # Validate OUTPUT references
  1253→    for out_name in output_names:
  1254→        if out_name not in signal_names:
  1255→            raise UndefinedNameError(
  1256→                f"OUTPUT references undefined signal: {out_name}",
  1257→                file=filepath
  1258→            )
  1259→        # Also check that OUTPUT is assigned somewhere (either as input or assigned)
  1260→        if out_name not in assigned_lhs and out_name not in input_names:
  1261→            raise UndefinedNameError(
  1262→                f"OUTPUT references undefined signal: {out_name}",
  1263→                file=filepath
  1264→            )
  1265→
  1266→    # Validate that all assigned signals (except inputs) are defined
  1267→    for lhs in assigned_lhs:
  1268→        if lhs not in input_names and lhs not in wire_names:
  1269→            # Already added as wire, but check consistency
  1270→            pass
  1271→
  1272→    # Also: wires must be assigned (already tracked via assigned_lhs)
  1273→    for wname in wire_names:
  1274→        if wname not in assigned_lhs:
  1275→            raise UnassignedSignalError(
  1276→                f"Unassigned wire: {wname}",
  1277→                file=filepath
  1278→            )
  1279→
  1280→    return declarations, assignments
  1281→
  1282→
  1283→# =============================================================================
  1284→# Expression Evaluator (for circuit evaluation)
  1285→# =============================================================================
  1286→
  1287→def eval_expression(expr: ASTNode, signal_values: Dict[str, int],
  1288→                    signal_widths: Dict[str, int],
  1289→                    filepath: str) -> Tuple[int, int]:
  1290→    """Evaluate an expression and return (value, width).
  1291→
  1292→    Args:
  1293→        expr: AST node to evaluate
  1294→        signal_values: Dict mapping signal names to their integer values
  1295→        signal_widths: Dict mapping signal names to their widths
  1296→        filepath: For error reporting
  1297→
  1298→    Returns:
  1299→        Tuple of (value, width)
  1300→    """
  1301→    if isinstance(expr, SignalRef):
  1302→        if expr.name in signal_values:
  1303→            # Get the full signal value
  1304→            full_value = signal_values[expr.name]
  1305→            width = signal_widths[expr.name]
  1306→
  1307→            if expr.msb is None:
  1308→                # Scalar reference
  1309→                return full_value, 1
  1310→            else:
  1311→                # Slice or single bit
  1312→                if expr.msb >= width or expr.lsb >= width:
  1313→                    raise IndexOutOfBoundsError(
  1314→                        f"Index {expr.msb}:{expr.lsb} out of bounds for signal {expr.name} (width {width})",
  1315→                        file=filepath
  1316→                    )
  1317→                # Extract bits from lsb to msb
  1318→                mask = ((1 << (expr.msb - expr.lsb + 1)) - 1) << expr.lsb
  1319→                slice_value = (full_value & mask) >> expr.lsb
  1320→                return slice_value, expr.msb - expr.lsb + 1
  1321→        else:
  1322→            raise UndefinedNameError(
  1323→                f"Undefined signal: {expr.name}",
  1324→                file=filepath
  1325→            )
  1326→
  1327→    elif isinstance(expr, Literal):
  1328→        return expr.value, expr.width
  1329→
  1330→    elif isinstance(expr, Concat):
  1331→        # Evaluate all parts
  1332→        parts_values = []
  1333→        total_width = 0
  1334→        for part in expr.parts:
  1335→            val, width = eval_expression(part, signal_values, signal_widths, filepath)
  1336→            parts_values.append((val, width))
  1337→            total_width += width
  1338→
  1339→        # Concatenate: first part is MSB
  1340→        result = 0
  1341→        for val, width in parts_values:
  1342→            result = (result << width) | val
  1343→
  1344→        return result, total_width
  1345→
  1346→    elif isinstance(expr, OpCall):
  1347→        op = expr.op.upper()
  1348→        args_results = []
  1349→        for arg in expr.args:
  1350→            val, width = eval_expression(arg, signal_values, signal_widths, filepath)
  1351→            args_results.append((val, width))
  1352→
  1353→        # Bitwise operators (width-preserving)
  1354→        if op in ('NOT', 'BUF'):
  1355→            if len(args_results) != 1:
  1356→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1357→                    f"Operator {op} requires 1 operand, got {len(args_results)}",
  1358→                    file=filepath
  1359→                )
  1360→            val, width = args_results[0]
  1361→            result = (~val) & ((1 << width) - 1) if op == 'NOT' else val
  1362→            return result, width
  1363→
  1364→        elif op in ('AND', 'OR', 'XOR', 'NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR'):
  1365→            if len(args_results) < 2:
  1366→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1367→                    f"Operator {op} requires at least 2 operands, got {len(args_results)}",
  1368→                    file=filepath
  1369→                )
  1370→
  1371→            # Check all operands have same width
  1372→            first_width = args_results[0][1]
  1373→            for val, width in args_results:
  1374→                if width != first_width:
  1375→                    raise WidthMismatchError(
  1376→                        f"Operator {op}: operand widths {width} and {first_width} don't match",
  1377→                        file=filepath
  1378→                    )
  1379→
  1380→            mask = (1 << first_width) - 1
  1381→            result = args_results[0][0]
  1382→
  1383→            if op == 'AND':
  1384→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1385→                    result &= val
  1386→            elif op == 'OR':
  1387→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1388→                    result |= val
  1389→            elif op == 'XOR':
  1390→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1391→                    result ^= val
  1392→            elif op == 'NAND':
  1393→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1394→                    result &= val
  1395→                result = mask & ~result
  1396→            elif op == 'NOR':
  1397→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1398→                    result |= val
  1399→                result = mask & ~result
  1400→            elif op == 'XNOR':
  1401→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1402→                    result ^= val
  1403→                result = mask & ~result
  1404→
  1405→            return result & mask, first_width
  1406→
  1407→        elif op in ('MUX', 'ITE'):
  1408→            if len(args_results) != 3:
  1409→                raise ArityError(
  1410→                    f"Operator {op} requires 3 operands, got {len(args_results)}",
  1411→                    file=filepath
  1412→                )
  1413→
  1414→            sel_val, sel_width = args_results[0]
  1415→            a_val, a_width = args_results[1]
  1416→            b_val, b_width = args_results[2]
  1417→
  1418→            if sel_width != 1:
  1419→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1420→                    f"Operator {op}: selector width {sel_width} must be 1",
  1421→                    file=filepath
  1422→                )
  1423→            if a_width != b_width:
  1424→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1425→                    f"Operator {op}: operand widths {a_width} and {b_width} don't match",
  1426→                    file=filepath
  1427→                )
  1428→
  1429→            result = a_val if sel_val else b_val
  1430→            return result, a_width
  1431→
  1432→        elif op in ('REDUCE_AND', 'REDUCE_OR', 'REDUCE_XOR'):
  1433→            if len(args_results) != 1:
  1434→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1435→                    f"Operator {op} requires 1 operand, got {len(args_results)}",
  1436→                    file=filepath
  1437→                )
  1438→
  1439→            val, width = args_results[0]
  1440→            if width < 1:
  1441→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1442→                    f"Operator {op}: operand width {width} must be >= 1",
  1443→                    file=filepath
  1444→                )
  1445→
  1446→            mask = (1 << width) - 1
  1447→            val &= mask
  1448→
  1449→            if op == 'REDUCE_AND':
  1450→                # Check if all bits are 1
  1451→                result = 1 if val == mask else 0
  1452→            elif op == 'REDUCE_OR':
  1453→                result = 1 if val != 0 else 0
  1454→            else:  # REDUCE_XOR
  1455→                result = bin(val & mask).count('1') % 2
  1456→
  1457→            return result, 1
  1458→
  1459→        elif op == 'EQ':
  1460→            if len(args_results) != 2:
  1461→                raise ArityError(
  1462→                    f"Operator EQ requires 2 operands, got {len(args_results)}",
  1463→                    file=filepath
  1464→                )
  1465→
  1466→            a_val, a_width = args_results[0]
  1467→            b_val, b_width = args_results[1]
  1468→
  1469→            if a_width != b_width:
  1470→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1471→                    f"Operator EQ: operand widths {a_width} and {b_width} don't match",
  1472→                    file=filepath
  1473→                )
  1474→
  1475→            return int(a_val == b_val), 1
  1476→
  1477→        else:
  1478→            raise ArityError(
  1479→                f"Unknown operator: {op}",
  1480→                file=filepath
  1481→            )
  1482→
  1483→    else:
  1484→        raise ValueError(f"Unknown AST node type: {type(expr)}")
  1485→
  1486→
  1487→def parse_input_literal(value_str: str, expected_width: int,
  1488→                        filepath: str, line: int = None, col: int = None) -> int:
  1489→    """Parse an input literal value and return the integer value.
  1490→
  1491→    Validates that the width matches the expected width.
  1492→    """
  1493→    if value_str in ('0', '1'):
  1494→        if expected_width != 1:
  1495→            raise InputWidthMismatchError(
  1496→                f"Input width mismatch: got 1 but expected {expected_width}",
  1497→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1498→            )
  1499→        return int(value_str)
  1500→
  1501→    elif value_str.startswith('0b'):
  1502→        bits = value_str[2:]
  1503→        if not bits:
  1504→            raise InputValueParseError(
  1505→                f"Binary literal must have at least one digit",
  1506→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1507→            )
  1508→        value = int(bits, 2)
  1509→        width = len(bits)
  1510→
  1511→        if width != expected_width:
  1512→            raise InputWidthMismatchError(
  1513→                f"Input width mismatch: got {width} but expected {expected_width}",
  1514→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1515→            )
  1516→        return value
  1517→
  1518→    elif value_str.startswith('0x'):
  1519→        hex_digits = value_str[2:]
  1520→        if not hex_digits:
  1521→            raise InputValueParseError(
  1522→                f"Hex literal must have at least one digit",
  1523→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1524→            )
  1525→        value = int(hex_digits, 16)
  1526→        width = len(hex_digits) * 4
  1527→
  1528→        if width != expected_width:
  1529→            raise InputWidthMismatchError(
  1530→                f"Input width mismatch: got {width} but expected {expected_width}",
  1531→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1532→            )
  1533→        return value
  1534→
  1535→    else:
  1536→        # Sized literal: 8'b1010, 8'hff, 8'd200
  1537→        match = re.match(r'(\d+)\'[bBhHdD]([\d01a-fA-F_]+)', value_str)
  1538→        if match:
  1539→            width = int(match.group(1))
  1540→            radix_str = match.group(2).lower()
  1541→            value_str_clean = match.group(3).replace('_', '')
  1542→
  1543→            if width != expected_width:
  1544→                raise InputWidthMismatchError(
  1545→                    f"Input width mismatch: got {width} but expected {expected_width}",
  1546→                    file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1547→                )
  1548→
  1549→            try:
  1550→                if radix_str == 'b':
  1551→                    value = int(value_str_clean, 2)
  1552→                elif radix_str == 'h':
  1553→                    value = int(value_str_clean, 16)
  1554→                else:  # 'd'
  1555→                    value = int(value_str_clean, 10)
  1556→            except ValueError:
  1557→                raise InputValueParseError(
  1558→                    f"Invalid literal: {value_str}",
  1559→                    file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1560→                )
  1561→
  1562→            if value >= (1 << width):
  1563→                raise CircParseError(
  1564→                    f"Value {value} too large for {width}-bit literal",
  1565→                    file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1566→                )
  1567→
  1568→            return value
  1569→        else:
  1570→            raise InputValueParseError(
  1571→                f"Invalid input literal format: {value_str}",
  1572→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1573→            )
  1574→
  1575→
  1576→def format_vector_value(value: int, width: int, radix: str) -> str:
  1577→    """Format a vector value according to the specified radix.
  1578→
  1579→    Args:
  1580→        value: Integer value
  1581→        width: Bit width
  1582→        radix: 'bin', 'hex', or 'dec'
  1583→
  1584→    Returns:
  1585→        Formatted string
  1586→    """
  1587→    value &= (1 << width) - 1
  1588→
  1589→    if radix == 'bin':
  1590→        return f"0b{value:0{width}b}"
  1591→    elif radix == 'hex':
  1592→        # Calculate minimum number of hex digits
  1593→        hex_digits = (width + 3) // 4
  1594→        return f"0x{value:0{hex_digits}x}"
  1595→    elif radix == 'dec':
  1596→        return str(value)
  1597→
  1598→
  1599→# =============================================================================
  1600→# Validation
  1601→# =============================================================================
  1602→
  1603→def validate_circ_file(filepath: str, declarations: List[SignalDecl],
  1604→                       assignments: List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]) -> Dict[str, object]:
  1605→    """Validate declarations and assignments, compute widths."""
  1606→
  1607→    # Track all declared signals
  1608→    inputs: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1609→    outputs: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1610→    wires: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1611→    all_signals: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1612→
  1613→    # Process declarations
  1614→    for decl in declarations:
  1615→        if decl.name in all_signals:
  1616→            raise DuplicateNameError(
  1617→                f"Duplicate signal name: {decl.name}",
  1618→                file=filepath
  1619→            )
  1620→        all_signals[decl.name] = decl
  1621→        if decl.sig_type == 'input':
  1622→            inputs[decl.name] = decl
  1623→        elif decl.sig_type == 'output':
  1624→            outputs[decl.name] = decl
  1625→        elif decl.sig_type == 'wire':
  1626→            wires[decl.name] = decl
  1627→
  1628→    # Track assignments
  1629→    assigned: Dict[str, Tuple[str, int]] = {}  # name -> (op, line_num)
  1630→
  1631→    # Build dependency graph for cycle detection
  1632→    graph: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}
  1633→
  1634→    # Operator arities
  1635→    op_arity = {
  1636→        'NOT': 1,
  1637→        'BUF': 1,
  1638→        'AND': 2,
  1639→        'OR': 2,
  1640→        'XOR': 2,
  1641→        'NAND': 2,
  1642→        'NOR': 2,
  1643→        'XNOR': 2,
  1644→        'MUX': 3,
  1645→        'ITE': 3,
  1646→        'REDUCE_AND': 1,
  1647→        'REDUCE_OR': 1,
  1648→        'REDUCE_XOR': 1,
  1649→        'EQ': 2,
  1650→    }
  1651→
  1652→    for i, (lhs, expr) in enumerate(assignments):
  1653→        line_num = i + 1
  1654→
  1655→        # Check LHS is valid
  1656→        if lhs not in all_signals:
  1657→            raise UndefinedNameError(
  1658→                f"Undefined signal: {lhs}",
  1659→                file=filepath
  1660→            )
  1661→
  1662→        signal_decl = all_signals[lhs]
  1663→        if signal_decl.sig_type == 'input':
  1664→            raise InputAssignmentError(
  1665→                f"Cannot assign to input: {lhs}",
  1666→                file=filepath
  1667→            )
  1668→
  1669→        # Check for multiple assignment
  1670→        if lhs in assigned:
  1671→            raise MultipleAssignmentError(
  1672→                f"Multiple assignment to: {lhs}",
  1673→                file=filepath
  1674→            )
  1675→
  1676→        assigned[lhs] = ('expr', line_num)
  1677→
  1678→        # Check expression and collect dependencies
  1679→        graph[lhs] = []
  1680→
  1681→        def collect_deps(node: ASTNode):
  1682→            if isinstance(node, SignalRef):
  1683→                if node.msb is not None:  # Has slice
  1684→                    # Check bounds against declared width if available
  1685→                    if node.name in all_signals:
  1686→                        decl = all_signals[node.name]
  1687→                        width = decl.width
  1688→                        if node.msb >= width or node.lsb >= width:
  1689→                            raise IndexOutOfBoundsError(
  1690→                                f"Index {node.msb}:{node.lsb} out of bounds for signal {node.name} (width {width})",
  1691→                                file=filepath
  1692→                            )
  1693→                if node.name not in ('0', '1'):
  1694→                    graph[lhs].append(node.name)
  1695→            elif isinstance(node, Literal):
  1696→                pass  # literals have no deps
  1697→            elif isinstance(node, Concat):
  1698→                for part in node.parts:
  1699→                    collect_deps(part)
  1700→            elif isinstance(node, OpCall):
  1701→                # Check arity
  1702→                op_upper = node.op.upper()
  1703→                if op_upper in op_arity:
  1704→                    min_arity = op_arity[op_upper]
  1705→                    if len(node.args) < min_arity:
  1706→                        raise ArityError(
  1707→                            f"Operator {node.op} requires at least {min_arity} operands, got {len(node.args)}",
  1708→                            file=filepath
  1709→                        )
  1710→                else:
  1711→                    raise ArityError(
  1712→                        f"Unknown operator: {node.op}",
  1713→                        file=filepath
  1714→                    )
  1715→                for arg in node.args:
  1716→                    collect_deps(arg)
  1717→
  1718→        try:
  1719→            collect_deps(expr)
  1720→        except (IndexOutOfBoundsError, ArityError):
  1721→            raise
  1722→        except Exception as e:
  1723→            raise CircParseError(
  1724→                f"Error in expression: {e}",
  1725→                file=filepath
  1726→            )
  1727→
  1728→    # Check for unassigned signals (only wires and outputs)
  1729→    for name, sig_type in [('wire', wires), ('output', outputs)]:
  1730→        for decl in all_signals.values():
  1731→            if decl.sig_type in ('wire', 'output'):
  1732→                if decl.name not in assigned:
  1733→                    raise UnassignedSignalError(
  1734→                        f"Unassigned signal: {decl.name}",
  1735→                        file=filepath
  1736→                    )
  1737→
  1738→    # Check for cycles
  1739→    def find_cycle() -> Optional[List[str]]:
  1740→        visited: Set[str] = set()
  1741→        rec_stack: List[str] = []
  1742→
  1743→        def dfs(node: str) -> Optional[List[str]]:
  1744→            visited.add(node)
  1745→            rec_stack.append(node)
  1746→
  1747→            for neighbor in graph.get(node, []):
  1748→                if neighbor not in visited:
  1749→                    result = dfs(neighbor)
  1750→                    if result:
  1751→                        return result
  1752→                elif neighbor in rec_stack:
  1753→                    start_idx = rec_stack.index(neighbor)
  1754→                    return rec_stack[start_idx:] + [node]
  1755→
  1756→            rec_stack.pop()
  1757→            return None
  1758→
  1759→        for node in graph:
  1760→            if node not in visited:
  1761→                cycle = dfs(node)
  1762→                if cycle:
  1763→                    return cycle
  1764→        return None
  1765→
  1766→    cycle = find_cycle()
  1767→    if cycle:
  1768→        cycle_path = " -> ".join(cycle)
  1769→        raise CycleError(
  1770→            f"Cycle detected: {cycle_path}",
  1771→            file=filepath
  1772→        )
  1773→
  1774→    # Return success info
  1775→    sorted_inputs = sorted(inputs.values(), key=lambda d: d.name)
  1776→    sorted_outputs = sorted(outputs.values(), key=lambda d: d.name)
  1777→
  1778→    return {
  1779→        'inputs': [{'name': d.name, 'msb': d.msb, 'lsb': d.lsb} for d in sorted_inputs],
  1780→        'outputs': [{'name': d.name, 'msb': d.msb, 'lsb': d.lsb} for d in sorted_outputs],
  1781→    }
  1782→

  1811→def detect_format(filepath: str, format_override: str) -> str:
  1812→    """Detect circuit format from file extension or override.
  1813→
  1814→    Args:
  1815→        filepath: Path to the circuit file
  1816→        format_override: One of 'auto', 'circ', 'json', 'bench'
  1817→
  1818→    Returns:
  1819→        Format string: 'circ', 'json', or 'bench'
  1820→
  1821→    Raises:
  1822→        UnknownInputFormatError: If format cannot be determined
  1823→    """
  1824→    if format_override != 'auto':
  1825→        return format_override
  1826→
  1827→    # Determine from file extension
  1828→    _, ext = os.path.splitext(filepath)
  1829→    ext = ext.lower()
  1830→
  1831→    if ext == '.circ':
  1832→        return 'circ'
  1833→    elif ext == '.json':
  1834→        return 'json'
  1835→    elif ext == '.bench':
  1836→        return 'bench'
  1837→    else:
  1838→        raise UnknownInputFormatError(
  1839→            f"Unknown input format for file: {filepath}",
  1840→            file=filepath
  1841→        )
  1842→

  1844→def cmd_check(filepath: str, json_output: bool, format_flag: str) -> int:
  1845→    """Execute check command."""
  1846→    # Check file exists first
  1847→    if not os.path.exists(filepath):
  1848→        if json_output:
  1849→            print(format_error_json(
  1850→                FileNotFoundError(f"File not found: {filepath}", filepath),
  1851→                'check'
  1852→            ))
  1853→        else:
  1854→            print(f"Error: File not found: {filepath}", file=sys.stderr)
  1855→        return 1
  1856→
  1857→    try:
  1858→        fmt = detect_format(filepath, format_flag)
  1859→
  1860→        if fmt == 'circ':
  1861→            declarations, assignments = parse_circ_file(filepath)
  1862→        elif fmt == 'json':
  1863→            declarations, assignments = parse_json_file(filepath)
  1864→        elif fmt == 'bench':
  1865→            declarations, assignments = parse_bench_file(filepath)
  1866→        else:
  1867→            raise UnknownInputFormatError(
  1868→                f"Unsupported format: {fmt}",
  1869→                file=filepath
  1870→            )
  1871→
  1872→        result = validate_circ_file(filepath, declarations, assignments)
  1873→
  1874→        if json_output:
  1875→            output = {
  1876→                'ok': True,
  1877→                'command': 'check',
  1878→                'format': fmt,
  1879→                'inputs': result['inputs'],
  1880→                'outputs': result['outputs'],
  1881→            }
  1882→            print(json.dumps(output))
  1883→        else:
  1884→            input_strs = []
  1885→            for i in result['inputs']:
  1886→                if i['msb'] is not None:
  1887→                    input_strs.append(f"{i['name']}[{i['msb']}:{i['lsb']}]")
  1888→                else:
  1889→                    input_strs.append(i['name'])
  1890→            output_strs = []
  1891→            for o in result['outputs']:
  1892→                if o['msb'] is not None:
  1893→                    output_strs.append(f"{o['name']}[{o['msb']}:{o['lsb']}]")
  1894→                else:
  1895→                    output_strs.append(o['name'])
  1896→            print(f"Inputs: {', '.join(input_strs)}")
  1897→            print(f"Outputs: {', '.join(output_strs)}")
  1898→
  1899→        return 0
  1900→
  1901→    except CircuitError as e:
  1902→        if json_output:
  1903→            print(format_error_json(e, 'check'))
  1904→        else:
  1905→            print(f"Error: {e.message}", file=sys.stderr)
  1906→        return e.exit_code
